title: Expand EvaluationResult with discriminated outcome types for autonomy
description:
## Source
Council review at `Data/Pages/council/blueprint-yaml-autonomy-council-2026-06-29.md` — Blocking issue B2

## Background
The current `EvaluationResult` (in `Agent.Core/Interfaces/ILlmEvaluator.cs`) returns `bool ShouldReplan` + `string Reason` + `string Suggestion`. This is too narrow for the autonomy design, which requires 6 distinct outcomes: stop, continue same goal, advance sequence, create follow-up goal, schedule next wake, recover/replan.

## Scope
Define and implement an expanded `EvaluationResult` (or `EvaluationDirective`) as a discriminated union type that supports all 6 outcomes. This is an additive, non-breaking change (current `ILlmEvaluator` interface can keep the old signature with an overload).

## Deliverables
- Discriminated union type for evaluation outcomes
- Updated `ILlmEvaluator.EvaluateAsync` with new return type (additive overload)
- Updated `AgentBackgroundService.TryLlmReplanOnStallAsync` to handle new outcomes
- Unit tests for each outcome type
- No regression in existing build-stall replanning behavior

## Exit Criteria
- All 6 outcome types are representable and testable
- Existing build-stall LLM evaluation still works unchanged
- `FollowUpPolicy` can produce a `CreateFollowUp` outcome
- Timer wakeups can produce a `ScheduleWake` outcome
